programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Kelle Bryan, Antony Costa, Alexei Sayle and Emma Sidi test their general knowledge skills on this week's House of Games.

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael heads north from Tokyo to Honshu's Tohoku region, known as the rice granary of Japan. In the city of Sendai, he learns about 2011's disastrous earthquake and tsunami.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of day 12 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: Twenty Twenty Six
programme.desc: Sarah's gone viral, and rumours about a famous footballer put everyone under pressure. Contains some strong language.

programme.title: Newsnight, followed by Weather
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the news stories of the day. With Victoria Derbyshire. Followed by Weather.

programme.title: Unspun World with John Simpson
programme.desc: John Simpson looks at the pressure on President Trump to find an end to the confrontation with Iran, examines the way in which the war in the Middle East has affected China.

programme.title: Extraordinary Portraits with Bill Bailey
programme.desc: A sub-postmistress who campaigns for justice after being wrongly jailed in the Post Office Horizon scandal tells her remarkable story.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship Extra
programme.desc: Highlights of the quarter-finals on day 12 of the World Snooker Championship in Sheffield.

programme.title: Beyond Paradise
programme.desc: An off-grid community is thrust into the spotlight when a land developer is terrorised by the image of a Green Man. As tensions rise, secrets surface that someone wants buried.

programme.title: Pilgrimage: The Road to Holy Island
programme.desc: The pilgrims are in the Scottish Borders on St Cuthbert's Way in the Cheviots, reaching spectacular views on Wideopen Hill.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: In the Cornwall countryside, Briony May Williams helps a Sheffield couple find a countryside home with room for their impressive collection of book and film memorabilia.

programme.title: Shift the Thrift
programme.desc: In Harrogate, former Traitors stars Sonja and Aubrey swap strategy for style, hunting vintage tweed and bold 80s fashion to raise money for a cat sanctuary.

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael heads north from Tokyo to Honshu's Tohoku region, known as the rice granary of Japan. In the city of Sendai, he learns about 2011's disastrous earthquake and tsunami.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty is adding a dash of Mediterranean flair and flavour by planting lemons in pots.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The big political issues of the day.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of day 13 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces classic recipes from some of the BBC's best-loved television chefs. The menu includes Nigella Lawson's sausage meatballs.

programme.title: Forest Elephants: Rumbles in the Jungle
programme.desc: Documentary about Central Africa's forest elephants, as new acoustic research sheds light on their social lives and unique vocalisations.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: In Kent, Jonathan Pratt unearths a horn beaker used by Winston Churchill.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Kelle Bryan, Antony Costa, Alexei Sayle and Emma Sidi test their general knowledge skills on this week's House of Games.

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael's bullet train takes him through the undersea Seikan tunnel to Hokkaido. He explores the southern port city of Hakodate, one of the first to trade with the outside world.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of day 13 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul and Bob fish the mighty River Trent for the barbel and welcome a very special guest.

programme.title: Newsnight, followed by Weather
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the news stories of the day. With Paddy O'Connell. Followed by Weather.

programme.title: On the Basis of Sex
programme.desc: The story of Ruth Bader Ginsburg's fight for gender equality. Contains some strong language.

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Six more amateurs work to secure a place in this week's quarter-final as they are put through their paces by Grace and Anna in the second heat of the week.

programme.title: Michael Jackson: An American Tragedy
programme.desc: Jackson dies while planning a comeback, and his estate races to monetise his legacy. Contains some strong language and upsetting scenes.

programme.title: Artemis: To the Moon and Back
programme.desc: The inside story of the Artemis II mission - filmed over three and a half years at NASA. How did four astronauts travel to the moon, going deeper into space than ever before.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: As the red and blue teams shop near Exeter with experts Ben Cooper and Gary Pe, Christina Trevanion meets a museum curator with a fascinating collection of shipwrecked Lego pieces.

programme.title: Shift the Thrift
programme.desc: In Abergavenny, couple Claudia and Luke clash over instincts and risk everything in a rain-soaked race, ending in a last-minute upcycle scramble and a tense live auction.

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael's bullet train takes him through the undersea Seikan tunnel to Hokkaido. He explores the southern port city of Hakodate, one of the first to trade with the outside world.

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: Zoe Ball is in Kettering to share with busy beautician Lucie some clever digital detox tricks that could improve family life, reduce stress and even boost her brain power.

programme.title: Ronan Keating's Wild Atlantic
programme.desc: Ronan goes horse riding on the beach with fellow boyband veteran, Shane Filan of Westlife, and sails over rough waves with his nephew Conall to visit the breathtaking Clare Island.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of day 14 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: Montell Douglas presents an appeal on behalf of Action for ME, a charity that provides support and advocacy for people of all ages affected by ME.

programme.title: The Himalayas
programme.desc: Documentary looking at the variety of wild animals inhabiting the Himalayas, from monkeys, wolves and bears to foxes, snow leopards and pheasants.

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts the quickfire quiz where six plucky contestants must stay out of the light to be alright. Can anyone beat the lightning and win the £3,000 jackpot.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of day 14 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Kelle Bryan, Antony Costa, Alexei Sayle and Emma Sidi test their general knowledge skills on this week's House of Games.

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael explores the history of Hokkaido's indigenous Ainu people.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of day 14 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.
